rationale: Chakravarti et al. (2006) is used in the conceptual development for both Prerequisite 1 and Prerequisite 2, not merely in a general literature list. The citing paper explicitly relies on the screening-effect and prescreening-neglect logic to argue that consumers will not carry precondition-cost information into the regular-price purchase decision. That mechanism is central to the paper's explanation of when conditional promotions reduce total sales. So the citation has a clear functional role in theory building and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Chakravarti et al.'s prescreening-neglect idea to a conditional-promotion setting. It uses that prior logic to justify a key mechanism: consumers screen on precondition cost for promotion eligibility but then neglect that information when evaluating purchase at regular price.
